name: Multiple interviewers + silent observer
description: One interviewer dominates speaking time and a silent observer lurks with webcam off. Raw speaking duration alone would pick the wrong person.

metadata.calendarInviteText: System design interview for Vikram Nair with Priya Mehta and Rohan Gupta. HR may observe.

payload.text: Thanks for joining. Let's start with introductions — our team runs the fraud platform. Can you introduce yourself?
Hi, my name is Vikram. I am currently working as a teaching assistant and I built a distributed job scheduler last semester.
Great. Tell me about how you would design a URL shortener. Could you walk me through the storage layer first? The role involves a lot of system design.
I would approach it with a key-generation service. I used a similar design when I worked on the scheduler project.
Next question. How would you solve hot-key contention in that store?
I solved a similar issue before — my project sharded counters and batched writes to smooth the load.
Interesting. Can you explain the trade-offs versus consistent hashing? Do you have any questions for us afterwards?
Consistent hashing rebalances better. In my experience the sharded-counter approach is simpler to operate at our scale.

notes: p1 speaks the most (including a 60s monologue) but must be penalized as a known interviewer with question-style turns.
p4 joins late, stays silent with webcam off — classic observer.
Expected outcome: p2 selected; p1 and p4 score low.
